questions.question:
Study the following program written in a block-structured language:

 Var x, y:interger; 
procedure P(n:interger);
begin
     x:=(n+2)/(n-3);
end;

procedure Q 
Var x, y:interger;
begin   
    x:=3;
    y:=4; 
    P(y);
    Write(x)          __(1)
end;
 
begin
    x:=7;
    y:=8;
    Q; 
Write(x);             __(2) 
end.

What will be printed by the write statements marked (1) and (2) in the program if the variables are statically scoped?
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
For the program given below what will be printed by the write statements marked (1) and (2) in the program if the variables are dynamically scoped?

 Var x, y:interger; 
procedure P(n:interger);
begin
     x := (n+2)/(n-3);
end;

procedure Q 
Var x, y:interger;
begin   
    x:=3;
    y:=4; 
    P(y);
    Write(x);           __(1)
end;
 
begin
    x:=7;
    y:=8;
    Q; 
    Write(x);           __(2)
end.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question: If \(f(x_{i}).f(x_{i+1}) \lt 0\) then
options:
A.

There must be a root of f(x) between \(x_i\) and \(x_{i+1}\)

B.

There need not be a root of f(x) between \(x_{i}\) and \(x_{i+1}\).

C.

There fourth derivative of f(x) with respect to x vanishes at \(x_{i}\).

D.

The fourth derivative of f(x) with respect to x vanishes at \(x_{i+1}\).
questions.correct_answer:
A.

There must be a root of f(x) between \(x_i\) and \(x_{i+1}\)
